sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the figure is now overlapping with the green frame. The figure is an upside-down "F" shape, and part of it is inside the green frame. The red blocks are to the left of the figure.
Let me check the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'R', 'G'):
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
print("\nTotal green:", sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'))
print("Total red:", sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
